_notes: These limits are ROUGH, UNVERIFIED APPROXIMATIONS of the token budgets behind Claude Code's
session (rolling ~5h) and weekly usage windows. Anthropic does not publish an exact token
figure for these windows (they are enforced server-side, sometimes with model-weighted
'token-equivalent' accounting that discounts cache reads relative to raw input tokens), so
token_gate.py can only ever produce an approximation by summing input/output/cache tokens
observed in local transcripts (~/.claude/projects/**/*.jsonl) and dividing by the numbers below.
The starting values here were picked to be roughly the same ORDER OF MAGNITUDE as a real
measurement taken on 2026-07-10 (one heavy multi-project week produced ~880K raw tokens in a
single busy transcript file and ~350M raw tokens summed across all projects in 7 days -- see
document-archive branch's archive/reports/ for the ticket report with exact figures). They are NOT derived from any
published Anthropic quota and will be wrong for a different plan tier (Pro vs Max 5x vs Max
20x) or usage pattern.
Calibrate these values against your own plan: run `/usage` in Claude Code, note the displayed
session/weekly percentages at a given moment, then run token_gate.py at the same moment and
adjust session_limit / weekly_limit / fable_weekly_limit until the percentages roughly match.
fable_weekly_limit is a separate, smaller budget assumption for Fable-model usage specifically.
CALIBRATED 2026-07-10 (KST): user-reported /usage readings (session 42%, weekly 9%, fable 8%)
were matched against raw dedup sums at the same moment (session 2,619,135 / weekly 355,883,749 /
fable 4,961,338 tokens) and the limits below back-calculated as raw/pct. Still a linear proxy:
the session window definition (mtime-latest file) and unweighted cache_read summing differ from
Anthropic's server-side accounting, so expect drift; recalibrate the same way when it grows.
